steps.title: Find the main verb
steps.content: The sentence needs one main action for the subject an ecological study. The core claim is that the study found one species' eggs to be longer than another's. That means the words about comparing egg lengths must describe which study is being discussed, so they need to be introduced as a restrictive modifier after study.

steps.title: Choose the grammatical modifier
steps.content: Choice A - study, that. This incorrectly places a comma before that, even though a restrictive clause introduced by that should not be set off by a comma. Choice B - study, which. This creates a nonessential clause and leaves the sentence structure awkward before found. Choice C - study. This leaves compared and found competing as main verbs. Choice D - study that. This correctly makes that compared the lengths of eggs produced by different bird species modify study, leaving found as the main verb. The answer is D.
